_comment: Schiltron receiving-the-charge demo, split out of the combined schiltron.json into a dedicated clip -- see demos/README.md. The player Infantry unit spawns ALREADY in Schiltron (scenario 'formation': 6 -- Unit.FORMATION_SCHILTRON) so the clip skips the Shift+O transition and its settling time entirely, sidestepping the unrelated spawn-facing-flip churn a unit's first move order can trigger. Two cavalry spawn close behind (above) the infantry; both cavalry AND infantry auto-acquire each other on spawn since they're within DETECTION_RANGE, so without a Hold order the infantry would walk OUT to meet them instead of standing its ground -- the tick-0/1 click+Ctrl+H hold order (before the cavalry can close) keeps the ring planted so it reads as 'receiving' the charge, not colliding mid-field (a brief ~20deg facing settle happens in the one tick before Hold lands -- harmless, nothing like the dramatic spawn-facing churn). The enemy AI auto-advances the cavalry into the rear -- no further player input needed. First contact lands around tick 170-200; by tick 320 the ring has held (still SCHILTRON, still fighting) while both sides show real losses -- the brace/offence-penalty trade-off. Camera holds on the clash. Deterministic (scenario + seed + scripted input). Verified via tools/demo/dump-state.sh: infantry position/bbox stay settled and IDLE/Hold from tick ~5 until contact, formation stays SCHILTRON throughout.
